questions.question: Why do objects fall towards the Earth?
questions.answer: Objects fall towards the Earth due to gravitational force.

questions.question: What determines the magnitude of gravitational force between two objects?
questions.answer: The masses of the objects and the distance between them.

questions.question: What happens to gravitational force if distance between objects increases?
questions.answer: The gravitational force decreases.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of universal gravitational constant?
questions.answer: N m² kg⁻².

questions.question: Why does the Earth not move towards a falling apple?
questions.answer: Because the Earth's mass is very large compared to the apple.

questions.question: What is meant by acceleration during free fall?
questions.answer: The increase in velocity of a falling object due to gravity.

questions.question: Why do all objects fall at the same rate in vacuum?
questions.answer: Because acceleration due to gravity is independent of mass.

questions.question: What is the relation between gravitational force and acceleration due to gravity?
questions.answer: F = m × g.

questions.question: Why is the value of g different at poles and equator?
questions.answer: Because the Earth is not a perfect sphere.

questions.question: Why does weight change with location?
questions.answer: Because acceleration due to gravity varies from place to place.

questions.question: What happens to weight on the Moon?
questions.answer: Weight becomes one-sixth of its value on Earth.

questions.question: What is thrust in physics?
questions.answer: The force acting perpendicular to a surface.

questions.question: How does pressure depend on area?
questions.answer: Pressure increases when area decreases.

questions.question: Why do camels walk easily on sand?
questions.answer: Their wide feet reduce pressure on sand.

questions.question: What causes buoyant force?
questions.answer: The pressure exerted by fluid on an object.

questions.question: Why does a bottle float in water?
questions.answer: Because buoyant force balances its weight.

questions.question: What determines buoyant force?
questions.answer: Density of the fluid.

questions.question: What happens if buoyant force is greater than weight?
questions.answer: The object floats.

questions.question: Why does an iron nail sink in water?
questions.answer: Because its density is greater than water.

questions.question: Why does cork float in water?
questions.answer: Because its density is less than water.

questions.question: What happens to apparent weight in water?
questions.answer: It decreases due to buoyant force.

questions.question: What is displaced fluid in Archimedes' principle?
questions.answer: The fluid pushed aside by an immersed object.

questions.question: Name one device based on Archimedes' principle.
questions.answer: Hydrometer.

questions.question: Why do submarines sink and float?
questions.answer: By changing their density.

questions.question: Why are foundations of buildings wide?
questions.answer: To reduce pressure on the ground.
